text: spoke of elite warriors called samurai . They were the elite of their society, trained from young ages, feared by their enemies. But it was the ronin who caught my interest. In Japanese, it meant wave man . They were soldiers without masters, seeking causes worthy of their skill. Wandering the lengths of their land, bound by nothing but their blades and how far their feet could carry them. It…spoke to me .” “How much of it spoke to you ?” “Enough to choose Ronin as my name.” He dragged the paper closer and wrapped the remaining food. His chair stuttered over the floor as he stood up. Gathering the other food, he placed it all in the refrigerator and returned to his seat . Lara’s brow was furrowed, her eyes fixed on him. Her expression was thoughtful, but there were still hints of wariness in her posture . “You’re not like the other bots .” “Doubt either of us have spent enough time around other bots to know for certain .” “I’ve been around enough,” she replied. “You’re different .” “And you’re different from the other humans I’ve dealt with.” It was an understatement, but he couldn’t put his complex reasoning into words; he still didn’t quite comprehend why she was different . Her expression shifted again. It was another subtle change, but he was recognizing them in her with more ease, even if he didn’t know what they entailed . “You mentioned your sister,” he said when she made no reply. “Is there anyone else ?” Lara dropped her gaze to the table. “Don’t think so. I remember my mom being sick a lot, but she went scavenging every day, anyway. One time, she just never came back. I was five, I think. Never knew who my father was, if he was dead or alive. Don’t think mom did, either .” “Cheyenne doesn’t seem a good place for a five-year-old on her own.” What had she looked like at that age? How much had she changed since then ? She leaned back, settling a hand on her abdomen. “I was crying one day, because my stomach hurt. Mom always told me to suck it up, so I never cried when she was around. But this time, I couldn’t help it. It just hurt so much.” Frowning, she moved forward again, folding her arms and settling them on the edge of the table. “Tabitha found me and asked why I was crying. She was a few years older, and had already been on her own for about a year. She scavenged as best she could, and I think some of the adults took pity and helped her out sometimes . “So, anyway, she sat down next to me and hugged me because I just couldn’t stop crying, not even to answer her. She knew, though. Even without me saying anything, she knew I was alone and hungry. She held me, and, after a while, she gave me a potato from her pocket. That…that was all the food she had, just then. And she told me we were sisters, and that she’d always take care of me .” She smiled softly, moisture gathering in her eyes. “I’ll never forget that moment. I think…I know it was the first time I ever felt like someone cared about me. The first time I ever felt loved .” Lara’s smile fell away, and she met his gaze again, brows low. “I need to find her, Ronin. She’s all I have .” The layered emotions in her voice and expression were more than he could decipher. Fear, sorrow, determination, affection…loneliness? Solitude was familiar to Ronin; he’d wandered the Dust for so long by himself, never staying in one place for long, never building lasting relationships . What would it be like to have a companion, to share his existence with another person ? Would it be like this ? “I’ll do everything I can , Lara .” She’d told him bots did what they said, and he intended to. Not because of programming, not because it was the nature of his kind…but because he wanted to help her. His primary desire had long been to determine his true purpose, but his wants were increasingly building regarding this woman . She closed her eyes and eased back in the chair, exhaling. Relieved. Did that mean she’d placed some trust in him? That she had faith he would follow through ? “Right,” she said finally, opening her eyes. “Guess with all you’ve done, I’d better keep my end of the deal. We doing this in here ?” Images of Lara dancing in her shack flashed through his CPU, every movement framed forever by the slitted door through which he’d watched. Even if she performed the same dance now, he knew every step would be subtly different, somehow reflecting her current state of mind . “Where would you be most comfortable ?” “This is fine.” She rose quickly and walked to the relatively clear part of the kitchen, fingers trailing over the countertop. “So…you got any music ?” “No. I thought you made your own .” “Of course you’d remember that .” “I remember everything .” Her cheeks reddened before she looked away. Everything . The image of her on the bathroom floor returned, unbidden, and he knew his choice of words had been poor . “Okay, then.” Keeping her back to him, Lara inhaled deeply, shoulders rising and falling . She swayed her hips from side to side and lifted her arms over her head, grasping an elbow with each hand. Bowing her back, she turned gracefully to face him, bringing her hands down to slide over her chest, waist, and thighs. She kept her eyes closed . Arousal stirred within Ronin; though her clothing did nothing to accentuate her body, she was an attractive woman, and he longed to touch her so he could experience her through a different sensory input . Still, seconds ticked
